ast.target: Insert captured content on the same line, immediately after the matched text (no newline added).

ast.target: Place the text at the end of the matched section instead of the top.

ast.target: Also include the section’s subsections (requires target to be a heading starting with #). Subsections are headings inside the section.

ast.target: The name of the variable used to store the AI Assistant output, i.e. {{value:output}}.

ast.source: The system prompt for the AI Assistant
ast.target: The system prompt for the AI Assistant

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Show advanced settings such as temperature, top p, and frequency penalty.
ast.target: Show advanced settings such as temperature, top p, and frequency penalty.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Sampling temperature. Higher values like 0.8 makes the output more random, whereas lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. The default is 1.
ast.target: Sampling temperature. Higher values like 0.8 makes the output more random, whereas lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. The default is 1.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Nucleus sampling - consider this an alternative to temperature. The model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. 0.1 means only tokens compromising the top 10% probability mass are considered. The default is 1.
ast.target: Nucleus sampling - consider this an alternative to temperature. The model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. 0.1 means only tokens compromising the top 10% probability mass are considered. The default is 1.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim. The default is 0.
ast.target: Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim. The default is 0.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics. The default is 0.
ast.target: Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics. The default is 0.

ast.target: Use multi-line input prompt instead of single-line input prompt

ast.target: Keep drafts when closing input prompts so they can be restored on reopen. Drafts are stored only for this session.

ast.target: Shortcodes for natural language date parsing. One per line: alias = phrase. Example: tm = tomorrow.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: This prevents the plugin from making requests to external providers like OpenAI. You can still use User Scripts to execute arbitrary code, including contacting external providers. However, this setting disables plugin features like the AI Assistant from doing so. You need to disable this setting to use the AI Assistant.
ast.target: This prevents the plugin from making requests to external providers like OpenAI. You can still use User Scripts to execute arbitrary code, including contacting external providers. However, this setting disables plugin features like the AI Assistant from doing so. You need to disable this setting to use the AI Assistant.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: The string used to join multiple LLM responses together. The default is a newline.
ast.target: The string used to join multiple LLM responses together. The default is a newline.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: The string used to separate chunks of text. The default is a newline.
ast.target: The string used to separate chunks of text. The default is a newline.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: The maximum number of tokens in each chunk, calculated as the chunk token size + prompt template token size + system prompt token size. Make sure you leave room for the model to respond to the prompt.
ast.target: The maximum number of tokens in each chunk, calculated as the chunk token size + prompt template token size + system prompt token size. Make sure you leave room for the model to respond to the prompt.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Merge chunks together by putting them in the same prompt, until the max tokens limit is reached. Useful for sending fewer queries overall, but may result in less coherent responses.
ast.target: Merge chunks together by putting them in the same prompt, until the max tokens limit is reached. Useful for sending fewer queries overall, but may result in less coherent responses.
